classifications.phrase: increase the cash rate target by 25 basis points
at 7 per cent is still too high
This further increase in interest rates is to provide greater confidence that inflation will return…
upside risks to the inflation outlook have increased
services price inflation is still very high and is proving to be very persistent
Unit labour costs are also rising briskly
Growth in the Australian economy has slowed
conditions in the labour market have eased
the path to achieving a soft landing remains a narrow one
Some further tightening of monetary policy may be required

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance focused on controlling persistent inflation despite economic slowdown, with multiple references to inflation remaining too high and upside risks, balanced against acknowledgment of moderating growth and easing labour market conditions.
